title: Reader API Caching, ETags, Preconditions, and Invalidation · Stakeholder Go Mission
summary: A disability-led civic research group working across shared and personal devices. They need tenant-safe subscriptions, stable cursor timelines, bounded search, keyboard-first safe content, reliable personal state, privacy-aware validators, and recovery from conflicting updates. Planned artifact: Deliver Accessible Private Research Reader with acceptance evidence, rubric defense, reflection, and a changed-constraint extension. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case p…
objectives: Require tenant scope in repository APIs, SQL predicates, unique keys, tests, metrics, exports, and administrative paths.
Build forward and backward keyset predicates that match sort direction, null policy, indexes, and limit-plus-one boundaries.
Generate bounded snippets from safe text or sanitize marked fragments and expose matched terms without unsafe feed markup.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and Go Commit to the mission model · Project Feed Go Tenant Query Proof
steps.instruction:
project case — A project Go team handles case 7959 during an isolated backup restore exercise. Build deterministic Go evidence to reconcile private cache classification, ETags, conditional reads, write preconditions, and invalidation; browser Go must remain deterministic and free of network, database, process, and host effects; then change one schema, constraint, transaction, upsert, migration, query, or search plan and preserve admitted state. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Feed aggregation and Go Commit to the mission model · Project Feed Go Tenant Query Proof.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for require tenant scope in repository apis, sql predicates, unique keys, tests, metrics, exports, and administrative paths. For Feed aggregation and Go Commit to the mission model · Project Feed Go Tenant Query Proof, keep Feed Go Tenant Query Proof connected to executable Go feed, XML, HTTP, PostgreSQL, schedule, API, reader, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.
